V32 Number Station’s Location questioned An article in Taiwan’s "China News Net" , a portal run by "Want Want China", questions the idea that a transmitter near Böblingen in southwestern Germany would be the "number station" transmitting coded messages in Persian. Shortwave signals are reflected by the ionosphere during propagation, resulting in multiple paths and deflection effects, making it practically impossible to accurately track a single location. Experts point out that determining a signal’s origin from a specific military base solely based on amateur radio direction finding is technically extremely unreliable. 短波訊號在傳播過程中會經電離層反射，產生多重路徑與偏折效應，實務上難以精確追蹤至單一地點。專家指出，僅憑業餘無線電測向即判定訊號來自特定軍事基地，技術上可信度極低 That said, no experts are quoted by name.   #Germany #Iran #shortwave #Taiwan #USA
